data.narrative:
VEIR is a Series B-stage climate and energy company founded by Tim Heidel that has raised $117M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 5 same-sector, same-stage private companies (Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, CarbonCapture Inc., and Cemvita) ranging from $120M to $500M in valuation, alongside one public comparable.

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression method, which applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $222M. The peer-median method, anchoring to the median valuation of $310M across the five-company comparable set, implies $310M. The peer range itself spans from $120M (Cemvita) to $500M (Pano AI), reflecting material dispersion within the cohort.

Triangulation of these two methods produces a valuation range of $189M to $356M, with a median point of $266M. The gap between the stage-progression result and the peer median reflects the market's current pricing of similar-stage climate tech companies above pure funding-progression mechanics. Key uncertainties include VEIR's relative technology maturity, capital efficiency versus peers, and market timing within an increasingly competitive climate-tech landscape. The width of the peer range itself—spanning more than 4x between floor and ceiling—underscores stage-appropriate valuation sensitivity to company-specific execution risk.
